action.reasoning: Observation: After 27 iterations, best nav_seek_success=0.02 (iter 24). The latest iteration (27) with conservative piecewise local + angle-modulated pathfinder got 0.0 and only 77 safe steps. The debug_early_term.py test (iter 25) showed that the proportional local controller causes early termination (~305 steps) because it moves forward aggressively in the 1.5-2.0m zone, allowing the human to close the gap to <1.0m before backup kicks in. In contrast, the SAFE piecewise controller (no pathfinder) survived full 750 steps with min_d=1.04 and 143 safe steps locally. Analysis: We need a controller that (1) uses the pathfinder ONLY for long-range wall avoidance so nav_to_pos_success stays high, (2) switches cleanly to a local piecewise controller that starts backing up BEFORE 1.5m (at 1.6m) to prevent proximity-based early termination, and (3) uses angle modulation so the robot doesn't walk forward while facing away. The pathfinder should be gated behind dist > 3.5m AND (geo > euc + 0.5 or dist > 6.0). Local forward speeds must be high enough to track a wandering human (0.4-0.85) but with emergency backup below 1.3m. Decision: Rewrite model.py with this hybrid design, run train.py, and execute a 3-episode local sanity test. Do NOT submit eval this iteration; use the local results to confirm no early termination and decent safe_steps before submitting in iteration 29.
